lessons.title: Misrepresentation
lessons.description: The core principle is this. A misrepresentation is a false statement of fact made by one party before or at the time of contracting which induces the other party to enter the contract.

lessons.title: Mistake
lessons.description: The core principle. At common law, mistake is narrowly construed.

lessons.title: Duress
lessons.description: The core principle. A contract entered into as a result of duress — illegitimate pressure applied by one party to coerce another into contracting — is voidable at the option of the innocent party.

lessons.title: Undue Influence
lessons.description: Biehler captures the essence: undue influence arises where the defendant has caused the plaintiff's judgment to become "clouded by domination," resulting in the plaintiff entering a disadvantageous transaction.
